metadata.title: County Property Tax Rates and Veteran Exemptions — AZ
metadata.description: County-level property tax rates, veteran exemption implementation details, and application information for high-veteran-population counties.

notes.tax_rate_format: Rate per $100 of assessed value unless otherwise noted.
notes.scope: Initial release covers counties with major military installations from states in state-benefits.json.
notes.update_cycle: Tax rates typically set annually by county boards.

property_tax._note: County rates only 1.26/100 assessed. Total includes city, school, special districts (8-12/100 assessed varies by location). Effective rate = total rate × 10% assessment ratio. Prior value 0.126% was county-only, double-counted assessment ratio.
property_tax._fix_session: S77 — corrected per URGENT assessment-capped states doc

disabled_veteran_partial._note: Amount adjusted annually. Applies to assessed (not market) value.

disabled_veteran_100_pct.eligibility: Totally disabled veteran (any disability); AZ resident; income ≤$34,301; property assessed value ≤$28,458
disabled_veteran_100_pct.note: Arizona does not offer a full exemption for 100% P&T veterans. This is a modest income/value-tested partial exemption (~$4,188 assessed value, adjusted annually). Not P&T-specific — any total disability qualifies.

property_tax._note: Pima County has higher total levy rates than Maricopa due to Tucson city and school district levies. New-buyer LPV = full cash value.

disabled_veteran_100_pct.applies_to: Primary residence up to exemption amount
